title: In-App Feature Feedback Bar
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want to provide feature-specific feedback through an in-app feedback bar, so that the product team receives direct input on individual features to guide improvements.
description:
A slide-up feedback bar appears at the bottom of the screen on specific feature pages, prompting users with "What do you think about this [feature name]?" and presenting thumbs up/down icons.

**Interaction flow:**
- Thumbs up: immediately shows "Thank you for your feedback" in white text
- Thumbs down: hides the initial question and reveals a multi-line text box for detailed comments, then shows the thank-you message after submission
- Once submitted, the user cannot change their vote

Each user can only provide feedback about a given feature name once. Feedback is stored in a new `feedback` database table.

**Initial deployment pages:**
1. Entity Relationship page — feedback appears on page load
2. Metrics page — feedback appears after the user opens the off-canvas detail panel
3. Migration page — feedback appears after the user opens the popup and reaches field mapping

The feedback popup renders behind off-canvas panels so users can continue working without interruption. To submit feedback, they close the off-canvas first.
acceptance_criteria: Feedback bar slides up from the bottom of the screen on designated feature pages
Thumbs up/down icons display with hover state (outline fills white on hover)
Clicking thumbs up immediately shows 'Thank you for your feedback' in white text
Clicking thumbs down hides the initial question and shows a multi-line comment text box
User cannot go back and change their rating after submission
Each user can only provide feedback for a given feature name once
Feedback is stored in the feedback database table with user, feature name, rating, and comments
Feedback bar appears on ERD page on load
Feedback bar appears on Metrics page after opening the off-canvas detail panel
Feedback bar appears on Migration page after reaching field mapping
Feedback popup renders behind off-canvas panels so users can continue working
ux_changes: New slide-up feedback bar at the bottom of the screen with thumbs up/down rating and an optional multi-line comment text box. Appears contextually on ERD, Metrics off-canvas, and Migration field mapping pages. Thumbs up goes directly to thank-you; thumbs down shows comment box first.
middleware_changes: Feedback controller handles rating submissions and enforces one-feedback-per-feature-per-user logic. Checks whether the user has already submitted feedback for the current feature name before rendering the bar.
data_model_changes: New `feedback` table created via migration, storing user ID, feature name, rating (thumbs up/down), optional comment text, and timestamp.
api_changes: Endpoint to submit feedback (feature name, rating, comment text) and endpoint to check if a user has already provided feedback for a given feature.
implementation_notes: The feedback table was created using a Laravel migration. The feedback bar renders behind off-canvas panels using z-index layering. The feature name is parameterized so new pages can be added by specifying the feature name in the trigger.
comment_summary: Waqar implemented the feedback bar on the ERD page first for review. Ryan requested thumbs up skip directly to thank-you (white text) and thumbs down show the comment box while hiding the initial question. Waqar updated the flow and extended it to Metrics and Migration pages. The overlay renders behind off-canvas panels so users can continue working.
